leave early in the week for Seattle.
where Rev. Hill will be pastor of
the University Methodist church,
and DR.. and Mrs. Gibson of North
Yakima, who will make their home
in this city. Mr. Gibson has been

AMETHYST CLUB MEETS.
The Amethyst club will hold its
second meeting of the season next
Tuesday afternoon with Mrs. Will
liam C. Smith at the Smith home,
SILT Browne street.


q Z z
DELEGATES TO FEDERATION


OF WoMEN's CLUBS.


Mrs. Y. A. Pfile and Mrs. Lyman
F. Williams have been chosen to be
sent from the Twentieth Century
club as delegates to the State Fed
Grafton of Women's Clubs to be held
at Walla Walla October 11-13.


Z & z


MARRIED LAST SUNDAY.
Theodore X. Guntley and Sadie A.
II. Hulett were quietly married last
Sunday afternoon at 4:30 o'clock by
the Rev. C. F. Stevens at the Golden
West hotel. They will make their
home at that place.


& 4 4


WELCOME" PARTY.


A welcome party will be given
by the Y. w. c. A. Tuesday evening,
September 81, in compliment of
Miss Ayres, the new religious sec
rotary. All the young women OF the
city are invited.


Y. W. c. A. HAS NEW


GYMNASIUM.


The gymnasium classes OF the Y.
w. c. A., under the direction of
Miss Ruth Fauble, will meet this
winter in the administration build
ing of the South Central high
school.

HUFFMAN-Roe5iNs WEDDING
Miss Bertha Robbins Of Fleming,
N. Y, and FEd b. Huffman were
married Thursday at l:30 o'clock at
the home of the brides aunt, Mrs.
Joseph X. Robertson, S519 Howard
street. Rev. w. j. Kramer read the
service.


After short honeymoon trip, Mr..
and Mrs. Huffman will he at home
at E411 Ermina avenue.


Mrs. Lindsey will receive the
members of the Bayyiew club Tues
day afternoon at her home in Pine
Grove Terrace.
